content.problemStatement:
Mike takes part in programming contests. His favourite topic is dynamic programming(DP). As he said, that he likes problems on DP, because "you spend a lot of time on thinking and a little time on coding".

In this problem you are to solve a version of the knapsack problem(link), one of the most famous examples of DP problem.

You are given **N** items, each has two parameters: a weight and a cost. Let's define **M** as the sum of the weights of all the items.

Your task is to determine the most expensive cost of a knapsack, which capacity equals to 1, 2, ..., **M**. A cost of a knapsack equals to the sum of the costs of all the elements of the knapsack. Also, when you have a knapsack with a capacity is equal to **C**, then you can fill it with items, whose sum of weights is not greater than **C**.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains an integer N, the number of items. Each of the next N lines contains two integers W and C, the weight and cost of the corresponding item.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the most expensive cost for each capacity from 1 to M, where M is the sum of the weights of all the items.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The maximum cost for each capacity from 1 to M is calculated and printed.
The maximum cost for each capacity from 1 to M is calculated and printed.
The maximum cost for each capacity from 1 to M is calculated and printed.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using dynamic programming. We create a dp array of size M+1, where M is the sum of the weights of all the items. We initialize all elements of the dp array to 0. Then, for each item, we iterate from M to the weight of the item and update the dp array accordingly. Finally, we print the dp array from index 1 to M.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * M) per test case, O(T * N * M) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(M) — the dominant structure is the dp array.

code.Python3:
import sys

T = int(sys.stdin.readline())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(sys.stdin.readline())
    weights = []
    costs = []
    for _ in range(N):
        w, c = map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())
        weights.append(w)
        costs.append(c)
    M = sum(weights)
    dp = [0] * (M + 1)
    for i in range(N):
        for j in range(M, weights[i] - 1, -1):
            dp[j] = max(dp[j], dp[j - weights[i]] + costs[i])
    print(' '.join(map(str, dp[1:])))
